response.justification: Candidate has strong overall software engineering experience (14+ years) and solid fundamentals in testing, architecture, and event-driven systems, but the role specifically demands a senior C++ developer with IEC 62443/SDL security expertise and industrial automation exposure. C++ experience is limited to roughly 6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis; the modern career trajectory is JavaScript/TypeScript, DLang, and Node.js. No evidence of MQTT, industrial protocols, IEC 62443, or Azure. Not a fit for this requisition.
strengths: 14+ years of software engineering across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded with strong craftsmanship/TDD discipline
Event-driven and high-throughput systems experience (Sociomantic micro-services handling millions of req/s, GISCollective)
Mentoring and technical communication track record (workshops, Selenium course, conference talks)
concerns: Minimal recent C++/STL work — primary C++ stint was ~6 months a decade ago
No demonstrated experience with IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial cybersecurity standards
No MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, IEC 61850/104, DNP 3.0, or industrial automation domain exposure
